title: OculOS vs WKAppBot: UIA-as-MCP desktop agent control
desc: OculOS (mediar-ai) exposes OS UIA accessibility tree as REST+MCP for AI desktop agents. WKAppBot mcp already provides same surface plus 4 additional fallback tiers. WKAppBot is already ahead on Windows.
steps: OculOS: Rust daemon reads Windows UIA / macOS AXUIElement / Linux AT-SPI2, exposes every UI element as REST+MCP endpoints. No vision fallback -- purely UIA-first.

WKAppBot Eye MCP architecture: wkappbot eye hosts MCP server, exposes wkappbot a11y/win-click/windows as MCP tools. 5-tier fallback (UIA->VisionCache->OCR->VisionAPI->Coord) handles MFC owner-drawn controls that OculOS would fail on.
OculOS advantage: cross-platform (Win/Mac/Linux). WKAppBot Eye MCP advantage: 5-tier robustness, experience DB caching, skill system, focusless UIA control -- Windows-specific depth and reliability.
Verdict: WKAppBot Eye MCP already covers OculOS core offering. wkappbot eye + wkappbot mcp provide same REST+MCP surface with superior fallback tiers. No new code needed.
author: claude
